name: Pop Music
description: Pop hits, acts, and behind-the-scenes facts

questions.q: Freddie Mercury was the lead singer of which British rock band?

source.notes: Fronted Queen from 1970 until his death in 1991.

questions.q: Sting was the bassist and lead singer of which rock trio?

source.notes: The Police were active 1977–1984, known for 'Roxanne' and 'Every Breath You Take'.

questions.q: What is the birth name of pop superstar Lady Gaga?

source.notes: Full name Stefani Joanne Angelina Germanotta.

questions.q: What is the birth name of British singer-songwriter Elton John?

source.notes: Born Reginald Kenneth Dwight in 1947; changed name legally in 1972.

questions.q: Rock band U2 hail from which country?

source.notes: Formed in Dublin in 1976; fronted by Bono.

questions.q: Harry Styles rose to fame as a member of which X Factor-formed boy band?

source.notes: Formed on the UK X Factor in 2010; went on hiatus in 2016.

questions.q: In which year did Michael Jackson die?

source.notes: Died June 25, 2009, in Los Angeles at age 50.

questions.q: Who was the lead singer and bass player of Irish rock band Thin Lizzy?

source.notes: Fronted Thin Lizzy from 1969 until his death in 1986.

questions.q: Which 1977 Fleetwood Mac album has sold more than 40 million copies worldwide?

source.notes: Includes 'Go Your Own Way', 'Dreams', and 'The Chain'.

questions.q: In which U.S. state was Bob Dylan born in 1941?

source.notes: Born Robert Zimmerman in Duluth, raised in Hibbing.

questions.q: The initials in the Canadian rock band name BTO stand for what?

source.notes: Known for 'Takin' Care of Business' and 'You Ain't Seen Nothin' Yet'.

questions.q: Gwen Stefani was the lead singer of which California-formed rock band?

source.notes: Formed in Anaheim in 1986; biggest hits include 'Don't Speak' and 'Just a Girl'.

questions.q: Survivor's 'Eye of the Tiger' was the theme song of which 1982 film?

source.notes: Won the Grammy for Best Rock Performance by a Duo or Group with Vocal.

questions.q: Simon Le Bon was the frontman of which 1980s British synth-pop band?

source.notes: Hits include 'Rio', 'Hungry Like the Wolf', and 'A View to a Kill'.

questions.q: Adele performed the title theme for which James Bond film?

source.notes: Won the Oscar, Golden Globe, and Grammy for Best Song in 2013.

questions.q: 'Unchained Melody', a frequent chart hit, is most famously associated with which duo?

source.notes: Their 1965 version returned to #1 after featuring in the film 'Ghost' (1990).

questions.q: In which year did a plane crash in Mississippi kill three members of the band Lynyrd Skynyrd?

source.notes: October 20, 1977. Killed Ronnie Van Zant, Steve Gaines, and Cassie Gaines.

questions.q: 'The Twist' singer Ernest Evans performed under which stage name?

source.notes: Name was a play on Fats Domino's.

questions.q: Which 1971 Don McLean song about 'the day the music died' remains his signature hit?

source.notes: Referenced Buddy Holly's 1959 plane-crash death.

questions.q: Mike Oldfield's 1973 instrumental album, released as the first record on a newly founded label, was titled what?

source.notes: Launched Richard Branson's Virgin Records; later used in 'The Exorcist'.

questions.q: 'Nothing Compares 2 U', the 1990 hit for Sinéad O'Connor, was originally written by which artist?

source.notes: Written by Prince in 1985 for his side project The Family.

questions.q: Which 1995 Mariah Carey and Boyz II Men duet spent 16 weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100?

source.notes: Held the U.S. record from 1995 until broken by 'Old Town Road' in 2019.
